data.title: Selling U.S. Assets is a 'Dangerous Bet,' Warns UBS CEO
data.description:
Long-term U.S. Treasuries, stocks, and the dollar are all falling on Tuesday, although UBS (UBS) CEO Sergio Ermotti has cautioned that selling American assets is "quite a dangerous bet."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"Diversifying away from America is impossible," Ermotti said in an interview with Bloomberg Television at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland. "The US is the strongest economy in the world."

UBS Sees Strong U.S. Potential amid Tariff Tensions

Ermotti added that both innovation and wealth continue to grow rapidly in the country, which have factored into UBS's planned wealth-management expansion in the U.S.

U.S. assets have faced pressure in recent days after President Trump announced tariffs on eight European countries that will remain in place until the U.S. secures a deal to acquire Greenland. The European Union is currently considering countermeasures, including a $108 billion tariff package on U.S. goods.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Iren Stock Plunges as Investors Flee Risk Assets over Fears of U.S.-EU Trade War
data.description:
Shares of Sydney-based Bitcoin miner and data center operator Iren Limited (IREN) plummeted over 6% on Tuesday. The stock was among several crypto-linked names and risk assets that fell on Tuesday amid rising U.S.-EU geopolitical tensions.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Other Crypto-Linked Stocks Are Falling?

Meanwhile, shares in Bitcoin treasury company Strategy (MSTR) and crypto trading platform provider Galaxy Digital (GLXY) — which also operates as a data center operator — also plummeted 8% and 6%, respectively, as of 12:53 p.m. EDT. In addition, companies directly tied to the digital asset market, Coinbase (COIN) and Circle Internet (CRCL), were also not spared, dropping about 5% each.

Moreover, Iren's competitors, New Jersey-based CoreWeave (CRWV) and Amsterdam-based Nebius (NBIS), also tumbled, falling by about 6% and 7%, respectively.

On the contrary, the price of gold hit a record high on Monday, as investors took shelter in the safe haven amid a brewing trade war between the U.S. and Europe.

Iren Rides into 'Transformative' Year with a Rally

Meanwhile, despite falling on Tuesday, Iren kicked off 2026 with a bang. Since the start of the year, its shares have surged almost 50%, adding to the over 300% rise seen in 2025.

H.C. Wainwright analyst Mike Colonnese believes that "the stage is set for a transformative year" at Iren. Colonnese argued that the AI cloud infrastructure company's  recent $9.7 billion five-year deal with Microsoft (MSFT) is yet to reflect in its shares.

By contrast, Goldman Sachs (GS) has said that some doubt remains about Iren's growth potential beyond the Microsoft deal.

Is IREN Stock a Good Buy?

Across Wall Street, Iren Limited's shares remain a Moderate Buy based on analyst consensus rating. This breaks down to nine Buys, two Holds, and one Sell issued by 12 analysts over the past three months.

In addition, the average IREN price target of $82.80 suggests about 48% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Citi Names Its Top 4 Chip Stocks ahead of Earnings Season
data.description:
Investment firm Citi (C) sees a strong earnings season ahead for semiconductor companies and has named Nvidia (NVDA), Broadcom (AVGO), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), and Analog Devices (ADI) as its top picks. In a note to clients, five-star Citi analyst Atif Malik said that he expects earnings estimates across much of the semiconductor sector to rise. He added that chips tied to data centers are the most attractive right now due to strong AI and server demand, followed by analog chips, which are coming off low inventory levels and weak margins.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Looking more closely, Malik said that the recent pullbacks in AI-related stocks have created appealing entry points for Nvidia and Broadcom. He noted that concerns tied to companies like OpenAI and Oracle (ORCL) have weighed on sentiment, even as demand continues to improve. According to Malik, computing and networking needs for next-generation AI models are accelerating, which should support upside for both stocks. He also pointed to comments from Nvidia at CES and a recent increase in long-term sales and spending expectations from TSMC (TSM) as additional positives.

On the analog side, Malik said that the market appears to be approaching a recovery, though it remains unclear how long it will last. Given that uncertainty, he sees Analog Devices and NXP as the most attractive options based on valuation. At the same time, he struck a more cautious tone on Qualcomm (QCOM). More specifically, he expects the stock to fall after earnings, partly due to worries about losing modem business from Apple (AAPL). In contrast, Malik expects NXP shares to move higher.

Which Semiconductor Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the four top picks mentioned above, analysts think that NVDA stock has the most room to run. In fact, NVDA's average price target of $263.44 per share implies more than 47% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: All Eyes on Netflix (NFLX) Stock Post Earnings
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) stock moved lower Tuesday evening, falling ~5%, as investors looked past a better-than-expected fourth-quarter report and focused instead on the company's outlook and strategic priorities.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Specifically, the streaming giant reported fourth-quarter revenue of $12.05 billion, topping Wall Street's $11.96 billion estimate and rising from $10.25 billion in the year-ago period. Earnings per share came in at $0.56, edging past expectations of $0.55.

Netflix also disclosed that it now has more than 325 million paid memberships worldwide, reinforcing its position as the largest paid streaming platform globally. For the full year, revenue reached $45.2 billion, slightly ahead of expectations and representing 16% growth.

Looking ahead, Netflix forecast first-quarter revenue of $12.16 billion, representing 15.3% year-over-year growth and coming in $1.62 billion above Wall Street's estimate of $10.54 billion. The company also guided for adjusted earnings of $0.76 per share, exceeding the consensus forecast of $0.66. Netflix further projected 2026 revenue in the range of $50.7 billion to $51.7 billion, signaling that double-digit growth remains achievable.

Despite the solid numbers, the market response reflected concern about what comes next. In its shareholder letter, Netflix signaled that it plans to ramp up investment in original content over the coming year, a move that will increase costs before delivering longer-term benefits. Engagement in the second half of the year was supported by a 9% increase in viewing of Netflix-branded originals, though this was partially offset by declining engagement with licensed, non-branded content.

The company said the drop in licensed viewing followed an unusually elevated period of second-run content during 2023–2024, when the WGA strike temporarily halted new production across the industry. As those licensing deals rolled off, Netflix leaned more heavily on its own content slate, underscoring that engagement at this scale must be continually earned rather than assumed.

Advertising is increasingly central to that effort. Netflix revealed that ad revenue topped $1.5 billion in 2025, more than doubling from the prior year, and said it expects that figure to roughly double again in 2026. Ads offer Netflix greater flexibility in pricing and monetization per household, but scaling the business also brings higher near-term costs and uncertainty around margins.

Adding another layer of complexity is Netflix's pending acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery. The company confirmed it has paused its share repurchase program in order to preserve balance-sheet flexibility ahead of the all-cash transaction. Management emphasized its cash-generating strength, pointing to $9.5 billion in free cash flow in 2025 and guidance for roughly $11 billion in 2026, signaling confidence in its ability to fund the deal. Still, the potential integration risks, regulatory scrutiny, and front-loaded expenses weighed on sentiment.

Looking at the consensus breakdown, the optimists out gun the doubters. Based on 26 Buys vs. 9 Holds, and 2 Sells, NFLX stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Given the $126.33 average price target, the shares are anticipated to be changing hands for a ~45% premium a year from now. (See NFLX stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: IBM's New Quantum Algorithm Targets Real-World Problems that Classical Computers Struggle With
data.description:
Tech giant IBM (IBM) recently highlighted a new quantum computing breakthrough that could help companies make better decisions. In a research paper published in Nature Computational Science, IBM researchers and collaborators describe a new quantum algorithm for solving real-world problems that involve difficult tradeoffs. Instead of optimizing for just one goal, the approach focuses on situations where multiple objectives must be balanced at the same time. According to IBM, this may be one of the clearest opportunities for quantum computers to show real, practical value in areas like finance, logistics, and energy.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, classical systems work well when there is one clear objective, but real-life problems often involve competing priorities, such as higher returns versus lower risk. To simplify things, classical methods usually turn those extra priorities into fixed constraints, which can lead to suboptimal results. The new quantum method, called quantum approximate multi-objective optimization, or QAMOO, takes a different approach by exploring many tradeoffs at once and producing a range of strong solutions rather than a single answer.

This is where quantum computers have a natural advantage. Indeed, they are especially good at quickly sampling many possible solutions. In addition, tests run on IBM Quantum hardware and simulators showed that QAMOO reached high-quality solutions faster than several classical techniques, even with today's imperfect machines. While further testing is still needed, IBM believes this method could eventually help decision-makers clearly see all their options before choosing the path that best fits their priorities.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 12 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $320.13 per share implies 9.9% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: IBKR Earnings: Interactive Brokers' Financial Results Beat on the Top and Bottom Lines
data.description:
Interactive Brokers (IBKR) has reported fourth quarter 2025 financial results that beat Wall Street forecasts on the top and bottom lines.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The U.S. brokerage announced earnings per share EPS of $0.65, which was ahead of the consensus analyst estimate of $0.59. Revenue for the final quarter of last year came in at $1.64 billion, which topped the $1.61 billion forecast on Wall Street. Sales were up 15.4% from a year earlier.

Management said the company continues to benefit from the stock market boom that has been ongoing for the better part of three years now, as customer accounts and the money held in them continues to increase. data.title: Why Bitcoin Is Sliding Toward $90K amid Trump–Greenland Tensions
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is sliding toward $90,000 as investors grow cautious amid rising geopolitical tensions. Comments involving U.S. President Donald Trump and Greenland have weighed on market sentiment, leading some traders to reduce exposure to riskier assets like cryptocurrencies. BTC is down 2% in today's trading, extending its losses to over 5% in the past five days.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What's Driving the Plunge?

Over the weekend, Trump warned he would impose a 10% tariff on imports from Denmark, Norway, Sweden, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Finland, and the UK starting February 1, with the rate rising to 25% on June 1 unless an agreement is reached on his proposal to bring Greenland under U.S. control.

Trump said on Monday that he plans to discuss Greenland during this week's World Economic Forum in Davos. While he did not name who he would meet, leaders from several European countries are expected to attend.

This led to negative sentiment around cryptocurrencies. Bitcoin dropped sharply, sliding from nearly $96,000 to just over $90,000 in the last few days. The second-largest cryptocurrency, Ethereum (ETH-USD), is also down nearly 6% over the past five days. Meanwhile, gold hit a fresh all-time high as investors rushed toward safe havens.

Bitcoin Volatile as Bank of Japan Rate Decision Looms

Investors also became nervous and sold Bitcoin ahead of the Bank of Japan's upcoming interest rate decision.

The central bank signaled it could raise interest rates if the economy and inflation stay strong, which pushed Japanese bond yields sharply higher. Rising bond yields make safer investments more attractive, causing investors to move money away from risky assets like Bitcoin.

Year-to-date, BTC is up by 5.54%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: UAL Earnings: United Airlines Post Mixed Financial Results, Forecasts Record Year Ahead
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) has reported mixed financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025, but said it could generate record earnings this year as travel demand strengthens.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Chicago-based airlines reported earnings per share (EPS) of $3.10, which was ahead of the $2.94 expected among analysts. Revenue in the October through December period totaled $15.40 billion, which matched the consensus forecast on Wall Street.

Management said the Q4 results were fueled by increasing demand for air travel, with sales rising for both premium seats and no-frills airline tickets. Looking ahead, the carrier said that it expects earnings per share of $12 to $14 in 2026, which is in line with the $13.16 consensus view of analysts.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Erase YTD Gains as Trump Intensifies Pressure on Greenland
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Tuesday, with both ETFs now in negative territory since the beginning of the year.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

President Trump hasn't backed down from his goal of acquiring Greenland and stressed the importance of the self-governing territory for U.S. national security. "As I expressed to everyone, very plainly, Greenland is imperative for National and World Security," he said in a Truth Social post. "There can be no going back — On that, everyone agrees!" Last week, he announced 10% tariffs on eight European countries that will remain in effect until the U.S. inks a deal to purchase Greenland.

Separately, Trump threatened to impose 200% tariffs on French champagne and wine after French President Emmanuel Macron expressed unwillingness to join the "Board of Peace" for Gaza.

Leaders across the European Union are set to meet on Thursday to discuss countermeasures against the tariffs, with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen promising a "unflinching, united and proportional" response. The bloc is considering a $108 billion tariff package on U.S. goods that was shelved last year after the two sides reached a trade deal. Another option is the anti-coercion instrument, which authorizes the EU to impose retaliatory tariffs, tax foreign companies, and limit market access in response to economic or political coercion from other countries.

Heightened geopolitical tensions have benefited safe-haven assets, such as gold (XAUUSD) and silver (XAGUSD). Both precious metals secured new all-time highs amid a weakening U.S. dollar and falling long-term Treasury prices.

However, selling U.S. assets is "quite a dangerous bet," according to UBS (UBS) CEO Sergio Ermotti. "Diversifying away from America is impossible," Ermotti said in an interview with Bloomberg Television at the World Economic Forum (WEF) in Davos, Switzerland. "The U.S. is the strongest economy in the world."

Looking ahead, Trump is set to meet with global leaders on Wednesday at the WEF and teased that he will deliver "a lot of messages." On Truth Social, he shared a screenshot of a conversation between himself and Macron that showed an invitation to a meeting with G7 leaders and representatives from Ukraine, Denmark, Syria, and Russia.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 2.06% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 2.12%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Are Cars About to Get Smaller? Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slides on the Idea
data.description:
Sometimes it seems like things combine in a fashion that suggests future developments. Legacy automaker Ford (F) is working on narrowing the affordability gap in cars. President Trump, meanwhile, is frantically advocating for small cars. Between the two, it suggests a certain path forward, but one Ford investors were not particularly pleased with. Ford shares slid nearly 3% in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trump recently noted that, perhaps, Ford might find something of a market in producing smaller cars, particularly those similar to the Japanese "kei" car. This would line up nicely with Ford's own plans to make cars more affordable. After all, a smaller car is generally cheaper to make than its larger equivalent, and a cheaper car can be sold for less money.

In fact, both CEO Jim Farley and Ford's Executive Chair Bill Ford are on the same page, planning to make vehicle affordability a top priority in 2026. This does not exactly line up with Ford's previously stated mission of "no boring cars," but a small car does not necessarily need to be a boring car. Ford revealed that the company has some plans in reserve to bring out more affordable cars, but has not announced these plans yet.

The Red Bull F1 Turbo Case

Another, more hair-raising, story recently emerged about a Ford Racing property that was nearly seized at customs. Specifically, it was a turbocharger that would have been part of a Formula 1 racer. The United Kingdom facility that built it had no place to test it, so they shipped it to the United States to let Ford handle the job. But the turbocharger ended up stuck in customs limbo, and Ford Racing had to get it out.

It turned out that the problem was a lack of correct forms, specifically, a manifest that answered certain questions about the turbocharger. In some cases, the customs operations are deferred as the parts in question would leave the country not long after arrival. But in this case, the necessary forms were not filed, and Ford had to move quickly to get the part out of customs' hands and onto Ford's test tracks.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 30.39% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.75 per share implies 3.62% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
